summary: The knowledge graph captures the voxlblade-builder project with solid depth on the damage calculation engine, buff/debuff subsystem, and data layers. The 15-step tour provides an excellent onboarding narrative that follows a logical progression from types → constants → data → engine → UI. However, the graph suffers from multi-batch inconsistency: 3 different node ID formats, 3 different display-name properties, and inconsistent path properties. 51 orphan nodes (18%) lack any edges — critically, BuffData.ts (1838 lines), DamageAnalyzer.svelte (~6700 lines), and many core damage subsystem nodes are isolated. 8 important source files are entirely missing. The edge count (308) is reasonable but import edges for the central orchestration modules (build.ts, DamageAnalyzer.svelte) are sparse relative to their actual import counts.
strengths: Comprehensive tour (15 steps) that follows a coherent architectural narrative from types through engine to UI — ideal for new contributor onboarding
Well-structured 7-layer architecture (Types → Constants → Data → Engine → State → UI → Config/Infra) that accurately reflects the project's dependency hierarchy

Excellent coverage of the buff/debuff subsystem: 20+ function nodes in BuffData.ts with detailed summaries, call-graph edges between assembly pipeline functions, and reads-edges to all major constant registries

Strong engine layer representation: build.ts internal pipeline (accumulateEquipment → finalizePerks → deriveResults) has complete call edges, weapon.ts has both standard and Monk paths documented, and enchant.ts covers all 4 exported functions
Good depth on crit.ts and defense.ts subsystems — internal helper functions are represented with proper call-graph edges
JSON data files have meaningful semantic edges (blades→handles = composes-with, essences→blades = parallel-to, all item JSONs→perks.json = references)
Node summaries are consistently high-quality with specific details (line counts, export names, formula descriptions, game-mechanic context)
Tags are well-categorized: game-mechanics, damage-calculation, perk-system, buff-system, data-access, etc.
The constants layer has thorough coverage of 16 sub-modules with proper barrel re-export edges from index.ts
CI/CD layer includes both build and deploy jobs with correct dependency edge
weaknesses: 51 orphan nodes (18%) including critical modules: BuffData.ts has 0 import edges despite importing from ~10 modules; DamageAnalyzer.svelte has 0 incoming edges despite being imported by App.svelte; all interface nodes (BuffDefinition, GrantedBuff, etc.) lack contains-edges to parent files

8 source files entirely missing from the graph: src/data/cdr.ts, src/data/defensivePerks.ts, src/data/draconicBuffs.ts, src/data/HealBoost.ts, src/data/Runebasedmg.ts, src/data/SummonData.ts, src/lib/constants.ts, src/lib/constants/rune-base-damage.ts
Node ID format inconsistency across batches: 'file:src/...' (batch 1) vs 'file::src/...' (batch 2+) vs 'file:::root-file' (batch 3) — fragments traversal queries
Node display-name property inconsistency: 78 nodes use 'name', 51 use 'label', 154 use 'title' — all serve the same purpose
Node file-path property inconsistency: 214 use 'filePath', 33 use 'path'
Edge type synonyms: 'imports'(76) vs 'import'(15), 'depends_on'(11) vs 'depends-on'(1), 'related'(9) vs 'related_to'(3) vs 'relates-to'(1), 're_exports'(1) vs 're-exported-by'(10)
20 edges have undefined type (no type property set)
25 nodes use 'kind' instead of 'type' (root-level config/doc files from batch 3)
21 constant nodes and interface nodes lack 'contains' edges to their parent file nodes
JSON data files (armors.json, blades.json, etc.) have no import-edges to the data-access modules (character.ts, equipment.ts, weapons.ts) that actually load them
Barrel modules (engine.ts, data.ts, constants/index.ts, modals/index.ts, ui/index.ts) have few or no 're-exports' edges showing what they expose
Complexity ratings are mostly 'moderate' (73%) — the earlier batch had nuanced simple/moderate/complex but later batches defaulted to moderate
DamageAnalyzer.svelte (~6700 lines) has 0 incoming edges from App.svelte and no outgoing import edges to the ~20+ modules it wires together
build.ts only has ~5 outgoing import edges but actually imports from 15+ modules (crit, defense, BuffData, TypedDmgBoost, etc.)
recommendations: Normalize all node IDs to a single format: recommend 'file:path' for files, 'function:path::name' for functions, 'constant:path::name' for constants, 'interface:path::name' for interfaces. Remove the redundant 'file:' prefix from sub-entity IDs.
Normalize all node properties: use 'title' consistently for display name and 'filePath' consistently for file path. Remove 'name', 'label', and 'path' aliases.
Normalize all edge types to a canonical set: 'imports', 'calls', 'contains', 'depends_on', 'related_to', 're-exports', 'uses', 'reads', 'references'. Remove the 20 edges with undefined type.
Add the 8 missing source files (cdr.ts, defensivePerks.ts, draconicBuffs.ts, HealBoost.ts, Runebasedmg.ts, SummonData.ts, constants.ts, rune-base-damage.ts) as nodes with proper summaries and edges.

Add missing import edges for BuffData.ts (imports from Boost.ts, constants/perks.ts, types.ts, constants/game.ts, enemyHpEffects.ts, debuffCombatEffects.ts) and DamageAnalyzer.svelte (imports from ~20+ modules). Add incoming edge from App.svelte to DamageAnalyzer.svelte.
Add 'contains' edges for all 21 orphan constant nodes and 13 orphan interface nodes to their parent file nodes.
Add import-edges for JSON data files (armors.json, blades.json, etc.) to the data-access modules (equipment.ts, weapons.ts, character.ts) that load them.
Add 're-exports' edges for all barrel modules to show their public API surface.
Improve complexity ratings: differentiate the 73% 'moderate' nodes into simple/moderate/complex based on actual line count, cyclomatic complexity, and branching depth.
Fix the 25 nodes using 'kind' instead of 'type' property (root-level config/doc files).
Add missing import edges for build.ts to crit.ts, defense.ts, BuffData.ts, TypedDmgBoost.ts, and other modules it actually imports from.
Add edges connecting the stat-boost system: statboost.ts → stat-conversions.ts has a duplicate edge (both imports and depends_on) which should be deduplicated to just 'imports'.

metrics.averageNodeQuality: high — summaries are specific, actionable, and game-mechanic-aware
metrics.edgeQuality: moderate — strong on call-graph and contains edges, weak on import edges for core modules and barrel re-exports
metrics.tourCoherence: excellent — follows logical dependency order from types → constants → data → engine → UI → config
metrics.layerAccuracy: accurate — layers match actual project architecture with clear dependency direction
